title: Companies join hands to collectively dunk on PlayStation's all-digital future Domino's pizza, KFC, and GameSir all threaten an end to physical production
summary: Companies mock Sony's all-digital future for PlayStation, threatening physical production
insight: Companies joining forces to protest against digital-only gaming models

title: Fire hydrant signs with Starlink antennas tested for emergency Wi-Fi in disaster-prone Japan existing widespread grid of street-level furniture can be used for communications network fallback
summary: Japan's Fire Hydrant Sign Co. has integrated Starlink antennas with its street signs to create a comprehensive Wi-Fi network, leveraging the existing infrastructure to ensure reliable communication in disaster-prone areas.
insight: Existing street infrastructure can serve as a fallback for emergency communications.

title: Turtle Beach KP7 Review: The accessory that does everything
summary: The Turtle Beach KP7 keypad offers versatility as a standalone macropad or accessory to the KB7 keyboard, but its price may be a deterrent.
insight: Adaptable accessory combines functionality and portability in a single device.

title: Intel reportedly adding two new 22-core SKUs with game-boosting cache to Nova Lake-S lineup 125W unlocked and 65W locked part rumored to be part of single-tile Core Ultra 5 tier
summary: Intel is reportedly adding two new 22-core Nova Lake-S SKUs with up to 144MB cache, a locked 65W variant and an unlocked 125W part.
insight: Boosted gaming performance with cache upgrade in high-end SKUs.

title: Intel confirms price hikes on select consumer and server CPUs citing supply costs and demand select Xeon processors now over $1,000 more expensive
summary: Intel has confirmed price hikes on select consumer and server CPUs due to rising costs and soaring demand.
insight: Increased CPU prices impact customers and businesses with high computing needs.

title: Inside the history of DRAM price-fixing lawsuits how HBM allocations could make a difference after two decades of failed cases
summary: DRAM manufacturers are facing lawsuits for alleged price-fixing and market manipulation. The lawsuits involve plaintiffs from the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California. The cases could have significant implications for the memory chip industry.
insight: HBM allocations could disrupt DRAM price-fixing cartels after two decades

title: Brand new Steam Machine hit with 'red line of death' GPU failure after playing No Man's Sky for just five minutes console 'bricked itself' following update in failure that echoes the horror of the Xbox 360's infamous RROD
summary: A Steam Machine user encountered a GPU failure known as 'Red Line of Death' after a brief gaming session and software update, echoing the notorious RROD of the Xbox 360.
insight: Faulty hardware and poor design can lead to catastrophic device failure.

title: HP OmniBook Ultra 14 review: Potent Snapdragon performance, great endurance, premium pricing
summary: HP's OmniBook Ultra 14 offers potent performance and great endurance, but at a premium price.
insight: Exceptional performance and battery life come with a hefty price tag.

title: Blackstone-owned QTS abandons planned world s largest data center campus after years of lawsuits 2,100-acre Virginia Digital Gateway project dies over a newspaper-notice technicality
summary: Blackstone-owned QTS has withdrawn its appeal for the Digital Gateway campus, ending the project. The 2,100-acre Virginia Digital Gateway was planned as the world's largest data center campus. The project's demise was due to a technicality involving newspaper notices.
insight: Regulatory technicalities can kill major infrastructure projects suddenly.

title: SK hynix, Samsung, Micron among semiconductor industry group lobbying against government intervention on domestic memory chip supply says move would worsen situation, suggests tax deductions on consumer electronics instead
summary: The SEMI industry group, representing SK hynix, Samsung, and Micron, opposes government intervention that would prioritize American manufacturers for memory chip supplies, arguing it would worsen shortages. Instead, they suggest tax deductions on consumer electronics. The move aims to ease the memory chip crisis and support the domestic market.
insight: Government intervention in chip supply may exacerbate shortages, not alleviate them.

title: BIGTREETECH Panda Sense Pro 8-in-1 smart air quality monitor works with Home Assistant and Klipper for 3D printers
summary: BIGTREETECH introduced the BIQU Panda Sense Pro 8-in-1 air quality monitor for 3D printing environments, integrating with Klipper and Home Assistant for automation and real-time monitoring of pollutants, temperature, and humidity.
insight: Enhanced air quality monitoring for safe 3D printing environments.

title: Robot maker Trossen sees hottest market in decades
summary: Trossen Robotics showcased robot arms powered by Intel Core Ultra processors and AI training at Computex, highlighting the company's advancements in robotics technology. This indicates a strong focus on innovation and integration of cutting-edge components. The robot arms demonstrated capabilities that may be applicable to various industries.
insight: Robotics technology is witnessing significant advancements with AI integration.

title: Microsoft reverses stance and adds Google account sign-in to Edge browser
summary: Microsoft Edge browser now supports Google account sign-in alongside Microsoft accounts, available on Windows and macOS.
insight: Enhanced interoperability between Google and Microsoft ecosystems

title: Jensen Huang's signed leather jacket could sell for up to $60,000 at charity auction
summary: Sotheby's will auction Jensen Huang's signed Tom Ford leather jacket, potentially selling for up to $60,000. The auction starts accepting bids on July 7. The jacket is associated with significant tech moments.
insight: Celebrity tech items can raise significant funds for charity auctions.

title: US homeowners installed a record amount of battery storage this year, and it's reshaping the grid
summary: US homeowners installed a record 673 megawatts of battery storage in Q1 2026, mostly in states with expensive electricity and supportive policies.
insight: Residential battery storage is transforming the US energy grid quickly.

title: In old PCs, what was the function of the "Turbo" button?
summary: The 'Turbo' button in old PCs increased CPU speed temporarily for demanding tasks.
insight: Optimized performance for specific tasks, not continuous use.

title: Tesla driver charged with manslaughter after car in FSD mode crashes into Texas home
summary: A 44-year-old Tesla driver in Texas crashed into a home while in FSD mode. The 2025 Tesla Model 3 left the street and damaged a 76-year-old resident's home on June 19. The driver, Michael David Butler, has been charged with manslaughter.
insight: Autonomous driving safety raises concerns with fatal incidents involving human error.

title: iFixit is creating a repairability scoring standard for US electronics
summary: iFixit is developing a standard repairability scoring system for US electronics, separate from its existing teardown scores. This new system aims to provide a formal, repeatable standard for rating device repairability. It will likely influence industry practices and consumer choices.
insight: Improved repairability standards can prolong product lifespan and reduce e-waste.

title: Video Game History Foundation founder says piracy remains the only viable game preservation method
summary: Video Game History Foundation founder Frank Cifaldi advocates for piracy as the only effective way to preserve video games due to game companies' refusal to make legacy content available.
insight: Piracy may be the only viable route to video game preservation.
